input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance on how to handle this in .NET 10.

In .NET MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed in newer versions. The recommended approach changed over time. Let me think about what the current best practice is for .NET 10.

In .NET MAUI for .NET 8+, the recommended approach for Android is to use the `Android:Window.SoftInputMode` in the Android manifest or programmatically. But the MAUI-specific API `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust` has gone through changes.
 
The reason `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` stopped working is that Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` starting in API 30. The modern approach on Android involves using `WindowInsets` and edge-to-edge display handling, which means I'd need to configure the window to not fit system windows and then manually handle the inset adjustments.

For .NET 10, MAUI likely has better built-in support through `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which handles keyboard scrolling automatically, but for more control on Android specifically, I'd need to work with platform-specific code to manage the insets properly rather than relying on the deprecated soft input mode.
